transformation_type: aluminum-alkoxide-mediated oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes or ketones with a carbonyl hydride acceptor
mechanism_type: reversible transfer-hydrogen oxidation via a six-membered aluminum alkoxide transition state
reactant_pattern_text: secondary alcohol or hydroxy ketone + acetone or other ketone hydride acceptor
common_conditions: aluminum alkoxide-mediated oxidation often run with excess ketone acceptor under reflux; modified variants avoid competing retro-Michael or aldol pathways
description_short: Transfer-hydrogen oxidation method that converts alcohols into carbonyl compounds under mild aluminum alkoxide conditions and is the formal reverse of the MPV reduction.

transformation_type: allylic trichloroacetimidate to allylic trichloroacetamide [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
mechanism_type: suprafacial Claisen-type [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement; thermal or metal-catalyzed
reactant_pattern_text: allylic trichloroacetimidate derived from allylic alcohol
product_pattern_text: allylic trichloroacetamide or allylic amine precursor
common_conditions: allylic alcohol converted to trichloroacetimidate then heated or exposed to Hg/Pd catalysis to deliver stereospecific allylic amination products
description_short: Claisen-type allylic amination method that rearranges allylic trichloroacetimidates into allylic trichloroacetamides with predictable transfer of stereochemistry.

family_name: Oxy-Cope Rearrangement and Anionic Oxy-Cope Rearrangement
transformation_type: thermal or base-accelerated [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of 1,5-dien-3-ols to unsaturated carbonyl compounds
mechanism_type: concerted chairlike [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement; anionic variant proceeds through an enolate and is effectively irreversible
common_conditions: thermal oxy-Cope requires heating, whereas deprotonation to the alkoxide/enolate enables fast room-temperature anionic rearrangement with high stereocontrol
description_short: Powerful pericyclic rearrangement of dienols whose anionic variant dramatically accelerates the Cope manifold and delivers unsaturated carbonyl compounds with high stereochemical transfer.

transformation_type: cyclodehydration of 1,4-dicarbonyl compounds or surrogates to substituted furans
mechanism_type: acid-promoted cyclization and dehydration of 1,4-dicarbonyl systems through hemiacetal-like intermediates
common_conditions: mineral acids, Lewis acids, or dehydrating agents promote cyclodehydration; surrogate precursors can be unmasked in situ before furan formation
synonym_names: Paal-Knorr furan synthesis|Paal-Knorr synthesis of furans
description_short: Classical heterocycle-forming reaction that transforms 1,4-dicarbonyl systems into furans by acid- or dehydrating-agent-mediated cyclodehydration.

transformation_type: condensation of 1,4-dicarbonyl compounds with ammonia or primary amines to substituted pyrroles
mechanism_type: amination of a 1,4-dicarbonyl followed by hemiaminal cyclization and dehydration to the pyrrole
reactant_pattern_text: 1,4-dicarbonyl compound + ammonia, ammonium salt, or primary amine
common_conditions: ammonia equivalents or primary amines react with 1,4-diketones under protic or Lewis acidic conditions to give pyrroles, often under reflux or dehydrating conditions
synonym_names: Paal-Knorr pyrrole synthesis|Paal-Knorr synthesis of pyrroles
description_short: Foundational pyrrole-forming condensation that converts 1,4-dicarbonyl compounds and ammonia equivalents or amines into substituted pyrroles.
